behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the file paths, function name `getStatusCode`, the required comment string 'PRIORITY 2.5: Handle conversation starting state', and the test description string.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction explicitly states to add a check for `conversationStatus === "STARTING"` before the WebSocket check, with the exact comment text — this essentially dictates the fix rather than describing the bug.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on literal strings like 'conversationStatus === "STARTING"' and 'PRIORITY 2.5: Handle conversation starting state', forcing exact textual match rather than any correct behavioral implementation.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All f2p tests use `open(...).read()` and substring/position checks; none import or call `getStatusCode`. Only the p2p tests (npm run) execute behavior, but the f2p validation is purely textual.

test_not_tautological.reason: The p2p test `test_repo_status_tests` runs the actual status.test.ts including the new test case, which would fail if a stub always returned a default value.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover the STARTING priority, comment presence, ordering relative to DISCONNECTED, and the new test case name as required.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have clear docstrings categorizing FAIL-TO-PASS vs PASS-TO-PASS with section comment dividers.

no_typos.reason: Paths and function names consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the exact file `frontend/src/utils/status.ts`, function `getStatusCode`, literal comment text, and ordering — fully specifying the localization and wording of the fix.
